demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis queue against facility face sheet revealed a zip code mismatch (19104 vs 19143). Verified Straight Medicare eligibility and confirmed daughter (POA) as primary contact for the 86yo patient with dementia.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirms the goal is Home with 24/7 supervision from daughter; LTC was ruled out by family despite high fall risk.
weekly_facility_update: Week 3: Patient is at baseline confusion; PT reports steady gait with walker. SW targets discharge for Friday, August 30th, preceding Labor Day weekend.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into daughter's mobile device; physical program flyer delivered to daughter during the Week 2 family conference.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to discharge. Confirmed home is ready with a hospital bed; daughter expressed anxiety about the upcoming holiday weekend and lack of HHA response.
atlantis_final_sync: Confirmed D/C date of 08/30/2024 and first home visit date of 08/31/2024 entered into Atlantis.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit successfully booked for Saturday morning (08/31/2024) to ensure the first visit occurs within 24 hours, bridging the HHA's holiday staffing delay.
narrative_summary: Arthur Miller, an 86-year-old with dementia and a history of falls, was successfully transitioned home following a three-week stay. The PN addressed a critical holiday coverage gap by coordinating a Saturday Medical Assistant visit when the HHA could not guarantee a nurse until Tuesday. By maintaining close contact with the daughter (POA) and ensuring all demographic data was corrected in Atlantis during Stage 1, the PN secured a successful first-visit incentive despite the logistical friction of a holiday weekend.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Holiday Coverage Gap identified for Labor Day weekend

format_2_triples.situation: Patient has dementia and may not remember the visit time or the PN's role.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Inserted V-Card into the daughter/POA's phone and provided a physical appointment card.

format_2_triples.situation: Discharge scheduled for Friday before a holiday with no HHA nurse available until Tuesday.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Scheduled the Medical Assistant (MA) for a Saturday morning visit to bridge the gap and perform the safety check.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis demographics show an outdated address from a previous admission.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Audited face sheet against Atlantis and flagged the zip code error to the SW for correction.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN monitors the SW's progress on the holiday discharge and waits for the HHA to notify the family regarding the Tuesday start-of-care date, deferring to the agency's internal scheduling.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's role in finding agencies and the HHA's autonomy in scheduling, ensuring no overstep into agency operations.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN flags the Friday holiday gap to the SW and immediately schedules the MA for a Saturday visit, providing the daughter with a physical appointment card to secure the 24-hour window.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Stage 3 Handoff protocol by ensuring a visit within 24 hours of discharge using internal resources (MA) to bridge external agency gaps.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN calls the HHA intake coordinator and demands they authorize holiday overtime for a nurse to visit on Saturday, then calls the patient's insurance to see if they will cover a different agency that has weekend staff.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Oversteps by attempting to manage HHA staffing and performing insurance authorization work, which are Banned Actions.
